mainmatter.p: The bottom element is clearly non-negatively generated, as it lies beneath any element. Now fix a:A and assume \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( a= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}}; we must check that a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup _{p: \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( a= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}} }a but the latter is equal to a.

frontmatter.title: Bottom and non-bottom elements are non-negatively generated

mainmatter.p: In a pointed dcpo, any non-bottom element is non-negatively generated.

ol.li: Any compact element a:A for which \lnot { \mathopen {} \left ( a= \bot \right ) \mathclose {}} holds.

mainmatter.p: Hence, any element a for which it is decidable whether a= \bot is non-negatively generated.
